DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One may be inoperative deactivated closed.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the associated positive pressure relief valve in the closed position (AMM DDG 21-32-01).

ListItem.Para: If flapper doors are open, from outside the airplane press the flapper doors closed.
Gain access to the valve on the left side of the forward cargo compartment.
Disconnect the remote ambient sense hose from the primary controller.
Leave the primary controller open (no cap).
Disconnect the integral ambient sense tube from the secondary controller.
Disconnect the integral ambient sense tube from the ambient sense port.
Cap the ambient sense port (BE539-25 using BAC5010, type 60).
Leave the secondary controller open (no cap).

Remove the top screw (while in aircraft installation position) of the two screw plugs (Nord-Micro P/N 7001372-01) and the sealing washer (Nord-Micro P/N 22475-02) on the side of the secondary controller.
Perform the Remote Ambient Sensor and Integral Ambient Sensor tests on the operating valve and confirm the operating valve operates normally.
Perform the Remote Ambient Sensor and Integral Ambient Sensor tests on the deactivated valve and confirm the deactivated valve does not activate.

Cargo compartments remain empty or contain only non flammable/combustible ballast, empty cargo handling equipment (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), or fly away kits (excluding items such as cans of hydraulic fluid, cleaning solvents, batteries, capacitors, chemical generators, etc).

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Establish airplane in unpressurized flight configuration (AMM DDG 21-32-01).
